title: gentianopsis ciliata subsp. ciliata
intro: Gentianopsis ciliata subsp. ciliata is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Gentian family, also known as the fringed gentian, characterized by its unique, fringed petals and blue to purple flowers. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant is valued for its bitter properties, which are believed to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and flatulence. The health benefits of Gentianopsis ciliata subsp. ciliata include its ability to stimulate appetite and improve digestion, as well as its potential to help lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. Gentianopsis ciliata subsp. ciliata can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
